headline: GOLD REBOUNDS TO $4,637; TRUMP RULES OUT QUICK IRAN DEAL, EXTENDING HORMUZ SUPPLY SHOCK
snippet: Gold climbed back toward $4,637 per ounce on Thursday (April 30), recovering from one-month lows near $4,550 after Trump signalled no imminent deal with Iran without agreement on their nuclear arsenal, prolonging the Strait of Hormuz disruption that has rattled global markets since mid-April; gold is up more than 25% since early 2025 but off recent highs above $4,700 as oil-driven inflation weighs on rate-cut expectations.

headline: COPPER THREATENED: China Halts Sulphuric Acid Exports — 20% of Chile's Output on the Line
snippet: China is set to cease sulphuric acid exports from May 2026, removing critical supply at the worst time for Chile — which depends on acid leaching for roughly 20% of its copper output; sulphuric acid prices in Chile have already jumped 44% in a month as Middle East conflict cut off sulphur shipments, and the China ban now eliminates the alternative source that Chilean miners relied upon.

headline: SILVER SLAMMED TO $73 — March Low — as Oil Spike Revives Fears of 2027 Rate Hike
snippet: Silver plunged more than 3% to $73 per ounce on Tuesday (April 28), its lowest level since late March, as surging oil prices from the Strait of Hormuz disruption amplified inflation concerns and prompted FOMC members to dissent against the easing bias; traders are now beginning to price in a rate hike by 2027, reducing the appeal of non-yielding assets — even as silver has surged more than 150% over the past year.

headline: WORLD BANK DRAWS LINE ON GOLD: Only 5% Gain Forecast for 2026 After 41% Surge Last Year
snippet: The World Bank's April commodity outlook projects precious metals prices will rise only 5% in 2026 — a significant deceleration after an estimated 41% surge in 2025 — as official sector demand and investor interest gradually moderate; the bank identifies the Middle East conflict and trade tensions as the primary upside risks that could push gold and silver beyond their forecast ceiling.

headline: VISIBLE GOLD AT 190M: Emperor Metals Extends Duquesne West 1.5km East; 1.46Moz Quebec Resource Grows
snippet: Emperor Metals (TSXV: AUOZ) hit visible gold at 190 metres downhole in drill hole DQ26-06 — roughly 1.5 kilometres east of the main pit at its Duquesne West project in Quebec — expanding the mineralized footprint well beyond the known deposit boundary; the property already hosts a 1.46Moz inferred resource at 1.69 g/t Au, and the company is combining 15,000m of new drilling with 8,000m of historical core resampling to refine the model.

headline: LITHIUM UP 50% YTD to 2-Year High as Energy Storage Boom Overrides Oversupply Narrative
snippet: Lithium carbonate prices in China have climbed to CNY 173,000 per tonne in April 2026 — nearly 50% above the year-start level and the highest in three months — as a boom in grid-scale battery storage bolsters long-term demand expectations; the rally was accelerated by China's April 1 reduction in battery export VAT rebates, which prompted manufacturers to front-load production, and has reawakened hopes for a sustained recovery in a sector battered by oversupply since 2023.

headline: RECORD Q1 M&A: Mining Deals Hit $21.6B — Strongest First Quarter Since 2023
snippet: Global mining M&A reached $21.6 billion across 121 transactions in Q1 2026 — the strongest start to a year since 2023 and up 34% from Q1 2025 — as capital continues to rotate toward assets that underpin critical mineral supply chains; full-year 2025 mining M&A reached $93.7 billion, the highest in 13 years, and strategic partnerships are emerging as the dominant deal structure for 2026.

headline: Rare Earth Stocks Rally on Supply-Chain Bets — USA Rare Earth and Aclara Resources Lead Move
snippet: Rare earth equities maintained their April momentum as investors positioned for accelerating supply-chain diversification away from China, with USA Rare Earth and Aclara Resources among the leading names; the sector's case was bolstered by China Northern Rare Earth raising Q2 2026 ore prices by 44% — the seventh consecutive quarterly increase and the largest single-quarter jump since China reformed its pricing mechanism in 2023.

headline: NexGen Breaks Ground This Summer at Rook I — Canada's Largest Uranium Deposit Enters Construction
snippet: NexGen Energy (TSX/NYSE: NXE) plans to begin full construction at its Rook I uranium project in northern Saskatchewan this summer, following final CNSC regulatory approval in March; the Arrow deposit could produce nearly 30 million lb. of U3O8 per year over the first half of its 11-year mine life, with uranium's term price holding at $80/lb and NexGen representing the largest shovel-ready uranium build in Canada.

headline: HAYWOOD SECURITIES: Gold Equities 'Still Cheap' at 9.6x Cash Flow Despite $4,600+ Gold
snippet: Haywood Securities argues that senior gold producers are trading at just 9.6x forward cash flow — only marginally above their five-year average of 8.35x — despite gold prices running above $4,600/oz and generating record margins; the firm forecasts gold averaging $4,906/oz in 2026 and $5,000 in 2027, calls current pullbacks 'an attractive entry point,' and highlights continued M&A as a catalyst for development-stage projects to re-rate.

headline: GoldSeek COT Report: Gold, Silver & USDX Speculative Positioning — April 24 Snapshot
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly Commitment of Traders analysis for the survey week of April 24 maps speculative net positioning in Comex gold and silver futures alongside the US dollar index — a key tool for junior mining investors tracking sentiment extremes that historically precede trend reversals in precious metals; the report arrives as gold pulled back from $4,700 highs amid oil-driven inflation concerns.

headline: FED RATE CUT PUSHED TO LATE 2026 as Middle East Oil Shock Rekindles Inflation Fears
snippet: A Reuters poll of economists now places the Fed's first rate cut in late 2026, pushed back from earlier mid-year forecasts, as the Strait of Hormuz closure has triggered what the IEA calls 'the largest supply shock on record' in oil markets; some traders have begun pricing the possibility of a rate hike by 2027, a macro headwind for non-yielding precious metals but a mixed signal for miners whose costs are USD-denominated.
